message:
chore(build): make clipboard feature opt-in, add local asupersync path patch

Two build configuration changes:

1. Clipboard feature moved from default to opt-in:
   The clipboard and arboard dependencies pull in platform-specific crates
   (X11, Wayland bindings on Linux) that increase binary size and compile
   time. Since clipboard paste is primarily useful in interactive desktop
   sessions and not needed for headless/RPC/CI usage, removing it from
   defaults trims the default binary footprint. Users who need clipboard
   support can enable it with --features clipboard.

2. Added [patch.crates-io] for asupersync:
   Points asupersync to the local sibling checkout at /dp/asupersync,
   ensuring the binary always builds against the latest local runtime
   changes without requiring a crates.io publish cycle. This is the
   standard development workflow for the asupersync+pi_agent_rust pair.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
